web_text: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ through the will of God, according to the promise of the life which is in Christ Jesus, to Timothy, my beloved child: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord. I thank God, whom I serve as my forefathers did, with a pure conscience. How unceasing is my memory of you in my petitions, night and day longing to see you, remembering your tears, that I may be filled with joy;
slang_text: It's Paul here, an apostle of Jesus Christ through God's own will, matchin' up with the promise of life that's in Christ Jesus, writin' to Timothy, my beloved kid: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord. I thank God, who I serve with a clean conscience same as my ancestors did. Man, I think of you nonstop in my prayers, night and day achin' to see you, rememberin' your tears, so I'd get filled up with joy;

web_text: having been reminded of the sincere faith that is in you; which lived first in your grandmother Lois, and your mother Eunice, and, I am persuaded, in you also. For this cause, I remind you that you should stir up the gift of God which is in you through the laying on of my hands. For God didn’t give us a spirit of fear, but of power, love, and self-control. Therefore don’t be ashamed of the testimony of our Lord, nor of me his prisoner; but endure hardship for the Good News according to the power of God,
slang_text: since I got reminded of the genuine faith that's in you, which lived first in your grandma Lois, and your mom Eunice, and I'm convinced it's livin' in you too. That's why I'm remindin' you to stir up the gift of God that's in you, from when I laid hands on you. 'Cause God didn't give us a spirit of fear — He gave us power, love, and self-control. So don't be ashamed of our Lord's testimony, or of me, His prisoner — instead, put up with hardship for the Good News, powered by God,

web_text:
who saved us and called us with a holy calling, not according to our works, but according to his own purpose and grace, which was given to us in Christ Jesus before times eternal, but has now been revealed by the appearing of our Savior, Christ Jesus, who abolished death, and brought life and immortality to light through the Good News. For this, I was appointed as a preacher, an apostle, and a teacher of the Gentiles. For this cause I also suffer these things.
Yet I am not ashamed, for I know him whom I have believed, and I am persuaded that he is able to guard that which I have committed to him against that day.
slang_text: who saved us and called us with a holy callin', not based on nothin' we did, but based on His own plan and grace, which He handed us in Christ Jesus before time itself even got started, and now finally got revealed by our Savior Christ Jesus showin' up — the one who wiped out death and brought life and immortality out into the light through the Good News. That's what I got appointed to preach, as an apostle and a teacher to the Gentiles. That's why I'm sufferin' through all this. But I'm not ashamed, 'cause I know exactly who I put my faith in, and I'm convinced He can guard what I've handed over to Him, right up till that day.

web_text: Hold the pattern of sound words which you have heard from me, in faith and love which is in Christ Jesus. That good thing which was committed to you, guard through the Holy Spirit who dwells in us. This you know, that all who are in Asia turned away from me; of whom are Phygelus and Hermogenes. May the Lord grant mercy to the house of Onesiphorus, for he often refreshed me, and was not ashamed of my chain,
slang_text: Hold tight to the pattern of sound words you heard from me, wrapped up in faith and love, in Christ Jesus. Guard that good thing handed over to you, usin' the Holy Spirit livin' in us. You already know this: everybody in Asia bailed on me, includin' Phygelus and Hermogenes. May the Lord show mercy to Onesiphorus' household, 'cause he refreshed me plenty of times and never got ashamed of my chains,

web_text: but when he was in Rome, he sought me diligently, and found me (the Lord grant to him to find the Lord’s mercy in that day); and in how many things he served at Ephesus, you know very well.
slang_text: but when he got to Rome, he hunted me down hard, and found me (may the Lord let him find the Lord's mercy on that day); and you already know good and well how much he helped out back in Ephesus too.

web_text: You therefore, my child, be strengthened in the grace that is in Christ Jesus. The things which you have heard from me among many witnesses, commit the same to faithful men, who will be able to teach others also. You therefore must endure hardship, as a good soldier of Christ Jesus. No soldier on duty entangles himself in the affairs of life, that he may please him who enrolled him as a soldier.
slang_text: So you, my kid, get strong in the grace that's in Christ Jesus. Whatever you heard from me in front of a bunch of witnesses, hand that same stuff over to faithful guys who can turn around and teach other folks too. So you gotta put up with hardship, like a good soldier of Christ Jesus. No soldier out on duty gets tangled up in everyday life stuff — he's tryin' to please whoever signed him up as a soldier.

web_text: Also, if anyone competes in athletics, he isn’t crowned unless he has competed by the rules. The farmer who labors must be the first to get a share of the crops. Consider what I say, and may the Lord give you understanding in all things. Remember Jesus Christ, risen from the dead, of the offspring of David, according to my Good News,
slang_text: Also, if some dude's competin' in a race, he don't get crowned unless he plays by the rules. The farmer who busts his hump oughta be first in line for a share of the crops. Think over what I'm sayin', and may the Lord give you the whole picture on it. Remember Jesus Christ, risen from the dead, from David's own bloodline, matchin' up with my Good News,

web_text:
in which I suffer hardship to the point of chains as a criminal. But God’s word isn’t chained. Therefore I endure all things for the chosen ones’ sake, that they also may obtain the salvation which is in Christ Jesus with eternal glory. This saying is trustworthy:
“For if we died with him,
we will also live with him. If we endure,
we will also reign with him.
If we deny him,
he also will deny us.
slang_text: which is why I'm sufferin' hardship, chained up like some criminal. But God's word ain't chained up, no way. So I put up with everything for the sake of the chosen ones, so they can score that salvation that's in Christ Jesus, along with eternal glory. Here's a sayin' you can trust: "If we died with Him, we're gonna live with Him too. If we hang tough, we're gonna reign with Him too. If we deny Him, He's gonna deny us too.

web_text:
If we are faithless,
he remains faithful.
For he can’t deny himself.” Remind them of these things, charging them in the sight of the Lord, that they don’t argue about words, to no profit, to the subverting of those who hear. Give diligence to present yourself approved by God, a workman who doesn’t need to be ashamed, properly handling the Word of Truth. But shun empty chatter, for it will go further in ungodliness,
slang_text: If we're faithless, He's still gonna stay faithful — 'cause He can't go back on Himself." Remind everybody of this stuff, orderin' 'em right in front of the Lord not to argue over words — it doesn't do nobody no good, and it just wrecks whoever's listenin'. Work hard to present yourself approved by God, a worker who doesn't gotta be ashamed, handlin' the Word of Truth right. But steer clear of empty chatter, 'cause it just drags folks deeper into ungodliness,

web_text: and those words will consume like gangrene, of whom is Hymenaeus and Philetus; men who have erred concerning the truth, saying that the resurrection is already past, and overthrowing the faith of some. However God’s firm foundation stands, having this seal, “The Lord knows those who are his,” and, “Let every one who names the name of the Lord depart from unrighteousness.” Now in a large house there are not only vessels of gold and of silver, but also of wood and of clay. Some are for honor, and some for dishonor.
slang_text: and that kinda talk eats away like gangrene, guys like Hymenaeus and Philetus — dudes who wandered off from the truth, sayin' the resurrection already happened, and wreckin' some folks' faith. But God's solid foundation still stands, with this seal stamped on it: "The Lord knows who's His," and, "Everybody who names the Lord's name oughta steer clear of wrongdoin'." Now in a big house, there's not just gold and silver dishes — there's wood and clay ones too. Some for fancy occasions, some for the trash.

web_text: If anyone therefore purges himself from these, he will be a vessel for honor, sanctified, and suitable for the master’s use, prepared for every good work. Flee from youthful lusts; but pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace with those who call on the Lord out of a pure heart. But refuse foolish and ignorant questionings, knowing that they generate strife. The Lord’s servant must not quarrel, but be gentle towards all, able to teach, patient,
slang_text: So if somebody scrubs themselves clean of that stuff, they'll turn into a dish for fancy occasions, made holy, ready for the master's use, all set for every good work. Run from youthful cravings, chase after righteousness, faith, love, and peace instead, alongside everybody who calls on the Lord with a pure heart. Don't get roped into dumb, clueless arguments, 'cause they just breed fightin'. The Lord's servant shouldn't be pickin' fights — he's gotta be gentle to everybody, good at teachin', patient,

web_text: in gentleness correcting those who oppose him: perhaps God may give them repentance leading to a full knowledge of the truth, and they may recover themselves out of the devil’s snare, having been taken captive by him to his will.
slang_text: correctin' whoever's fightin' him, gentle about it — maybe God'll hand 'em repentance that leads to knowin' the truth fully, and they'll snap outta the devil's trap, since they got taken captive to do whatever he wants.

web_text: But know this, that in the last days, grievous times will come. For men will be lovers of self, lovers of money, boastful, arrogant, blasphemers, disobedient to parents, unthankful, unholy, without natural affection, unforgiving, slanderers, without self-control, fierce, not lovers of good, traitors, headstrong, conceited, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God;
slang_text: But know this: in the last days, brutal times are comin'. Folks are gonna be into themselves, into money, braggin', puffed up, blasphemin', disobeyin' their parents, ungrateful, unholy, cold-hearted, unforgivin', trash-talkin', outta control, vicious, hatin' anything good, backstabbers, stubborn, stuck-up, chasin' pleasure instead of chasin' God;

web_text: holding a form of godliness, but having denied its power. Turn away from these, also. For some of these are people who creep into houses, and take captive gullible women loaded down with sins, led away by various lusts, always learning, and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth. Even as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so do these also oppose the truth; men corrupted in mind, who concerning the faith are rejected.
slang_text: keepin' up some fake godly act while denyin' the real power behind it. Turn away from folks like that too. 'Cause some of 'em sneak into houses and take captive gullible women loaded down with sins, dragged around by all kinds of cravings, always learnin' somethin' new but never able to actually land on the truth. Same as Jannes and Jambres went up against Moses, these guys go up against the truth too — minds totally corrupted, rejected when it comes to the faith.

web_text: But they will proceed no further. For their folly will be evident to all men, as theirs also came to be. But you did follow my teaching, conduct, purpose, faith, patience, love, steadfastness, persecutions, and sufferings: those things that happened to me at Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra. I endured those persecutions. The Lord delivered me out of them all. Yes, and all who desire to live godly in Christ Jesus will suffer persecution.
slang_text: But they're not gonna get far. Their bogus foolishness's gonna get exposed to everybody, same as it did with those other guys. But you already followed my teachin', my conduct, my purpose, my faith, my patience, my love, my steadiness, my persecutions, my sufferin' — all that stuff that happened to me back in Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra. I put up with those persecutions. The Lord pulled me out of every single one. Yeah, and everybody who wants to live godly in Christ Jesus is gonna suffer persecution too.

web_text: But evil men and impostors will grow worse and worse, deceiving and being deceived. But you remain in the things which you have learned and have been assured of, knowing from whom you have learned them. From infancy, you have known the holy Scriptures which are able to make you wise for salvation through faith, which is in Christ Jesus. Every Scripture is God-breathed and profitable for teaching, for reproof, for correction, and for instruction in righteousness,
slang_text: But evil guys and con artists are just gonna get worse and worse, conning folks and gettin' conned themselves. But you stay put in what you already learned, and what you're sure of, knowin' exactly who you learned it from. Since you were a little kid, you've known the holy Scriptures, which can make you wise for salvation through faith in Christ Jesus. Every single Scripture's breathed out by God and useful for teachin', for callin' stuff out, for correction, for trainin' folks in righteousness,

web_text: that the man of God may be complete, thoroughly equipped for every good work.
slang_text: so a person of God can end up complete, totally geared up for every good work out there.

web_text: I command you therefore before God and the Lord Jesus Christ, who will judge the living and the dead at his appearing and his Kingdom: preach the word; be urgent in season and out of season; reprove, rebuke, and exhort, with all patience and teaching. For the time will come when they will not listen to the sound doctrine, but, having itching ears, will heap up for themselves teachers after their own lusts; and will turn away their ears from the truth, and turn away to fables.
slang_text: So I'm orderin' you, right in front of God and the Lord Jesus Christ, who's gonna judge the livin' and the dead when He shows up with His Kingdom: preach the word; stay on it whether it's convenient or not; call stuff out, rebuke, encourage, with total patience and teachin'. 'Cause the time's comin' when folks won't put up with sound doctrine no more — instead, itchin' for somethin' new, they'll stack up teachers who tell 'em whatever they wanna hear, and turn their ears away from the truth, chasin' after made-up fables instead.

web_text: But you be sober in all things, suffer hardship, do the work of an evangelist, and fulfill your ministry. For I am already being offered, and the time of my departure has come. I have fought the good fight. I have finished the course. I have kept the faith. From now on, there is stored up for me the crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous judge, will give to me on that day; and not to me only, but also to all those who have loved his appearing.
slang_text: But you, stay level-headed in everything, put up with hardship, do the work of an evangelist, finish out your ministry. 'Cause I'm already gettin' poured out like an offerin', and the time for me to head out has come. I fought the good fight. I finished the race. I kept the faith. From here on out, there's a crown of righteousness stored up for me, which the Lord, the righteous judge, is gonna hand me on that day — and not just me, but everybody who's loved His appearin'.

web_text: Be diligent to come to me soon, for Demas left me, having loved this present world, and went to Thessalonica; Crescens to Galatia, and Titus to Dalmatia. Only Luke is with me. Take Mark, and bring him with you, for he is useful to me for service. But I sent Tychicus to Ephesus.
slang_text: Hustle and come see me soon, 'cause Demas bailed on me, fallin' in love with this present world, and headed off to Thessalonica; Crescens went to Galatia, Titus to Dalmatia. Only Luke's still with me. Grab Mark and bring him with you, 'cause he's real useful to me for the work. I sent Tychicus off to Ephesus.

web_text: Bring the cloak that I left at Troas with Carpus when you come, and the books, especially the parchments. Alexander, the coppersmith, did much evil to me. The Lord will repay him according to his deeds, of whom you also must beware; for he greatly opposed our words. At my first defense, no one came to help me, but all left me. May it not be held against them.
slang_text: Bring that cloak I left in Troas with Carpus when you come, plus the books, especially the parchments. Alexander the coppersmith did a ton of evil against me. The Lord's gonna pay him back for what he did — watch out for him too, 'cause he fought hard against everything we stand for. At my first defense, nobody showed up to help me, everybody bailed on me. May it not get held against 'em.

web_text: But the Lord stood by me, and strengthened me, that through me the message might be fully proclaimed, and that all the Gentiles might hear; and I was delivered out of the mouth of the lion. And the Lord will deliver me from every evil work, and will preserve me for his heavenly Kingdom; to whom be the glory forever and ever. Amen. Greet Prisca and Aquila, and the house of Onesiphorus. Erastus remained at Corinth, but I left Trophimus at Miletus sick.
slang_text: But the Lord stuck by me and pumped me up strong, so the message could get proclaimed loud and clear through me, so every Gentile could hear it — and I got rescued right outta the lion's mouth. And the Lord's gonna rescue me from every evil thing, and keep me safe for His heavenly Kingdom. To Him be glory forever and ever. Amen. Greet Prisca and Aquila, and Onesiphorus' household. Erastus stayed back in Corinth, and I left Trophimus sick in Miletus.

web_text: Be diligent to come before winter. Eubulus salutes you, as do Pudens, Linus, Claudia, and all the brothers. The Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Grace be with you. Amen.
slang_text: Hustle and try to get here before winter hits. Eubulus sends his greetin's, and so do Pudens, Linus, Claudia, and all the brothers. The Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Grace be with you. Amen.
